en_definition: a place where two or more roads meet and cross each other
example1: Turn right at the crossroads and you will see the museum.
example1_ru: Поверните направо на перекрёстке, и вы увидите музей.
example2: The taxi stopped at the crossroads waiting for the light.

en_definition: a hard surface where people walk beside a street
example1: Please walk on the pavement, not on the road.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, идите по тротуару, а не по дороге.
example2: The university students sat on the pavement eating lunch.

en_definition: a narrow passage between buildings or walls
example1: The restaurant is hidden in a small alley behind the main street.
example1_ru: Ресторан спрятан в маленьком переулке за главной улицей.
example2: Children played in the alley near their apartment building.

en_definition: a place where roads or railway lines meet or join
example1: Exit at the next junction to reach the airport.
example1_ru: Съезжайте на следующей развязке, чтобы добраться до аэропорта.
example2: The train accident happened at a railway junction.

en_definition: a raised area in a train or bus station where passengers get on and off
example1: The train leaves from platform three in ten minutes.
example1_ru: Поезд отправляется с третьей платформы через десять минут.
example2: We waited on the platform for our train to arrive.

en_definition: the money you pay to travel on a bus, train, or plane
example1: The bus fare increased this month to five dollars.
example1_ru: В этом месяце проезд в автобусе подорожал до пяти долларов.
example2: I need to check the flight fare before booking.

en_definition: a machine that sells tickets for travel
example1: I bought my metro ticket from the ticket machine near the entrance.
example1_ru: Я купил билет на метро в автомате у входа.
example2: The ticket machine at the station was not working yesterday.

en_definition: to register at a hotel or airport and give your luggage
example1: We need to check in at the airport two hours before the flight.
example1_ru: Нам нужно пройти регистрацию в аэропорту за два часа до вылета.
example2: The hotel guests check in after three o'clock in the afternoon.

en_definition: a situation when something does not happen at the expected time
example1: The train has a thirty-minute delay due to bad weather.
example1_ru: Поезд задерживается на тридцать минут из-за плохой погоды.
example2: There was no delay this time, and we arrived on schedule.

en_definition: bags and suitcases that you carry when travelling
example1: Please put your luggage on the rack above your seat.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, положите багаж на полку над сиденьем.
example2: The airline lost my luggage on the way to Madrid.

en_definition: a paper showing that you paid for something
example1: Always ask for a receipt when you buy something.
example1_ru: Всегда просите чек, когда что-то покупаете.
example2: I kept the receipt to return the broken lamp to the shop.

en_definition: money given back to you when you return something you bought
example1: If the shirt does not fit, you can ask for a refund.
example1_ru: Если рубашка не подойдёт по размеру, вы можете попросить возврат денег.
example2: The store promised a full refund within seven days.

en_definition: a reduction in price for something
example1: The bookstore offers a ten percent discount for students.
example1_ru: Книжный магазин предлагает скидку десять процентов для студентов.
example2: I got a nice discount because I bought five notebooks.

en_definition: to put on clothes to see if they fit and look good
example1: Can I try on these shoes to see if they are comfortable?
example1_ru: Можно я примерю эти туфли, чтобы понять, удобные ли они?
example2: My sister tried on five dresses before choosing one.

en_definition: something bought at a good price that is cheaper than usual
example1: That coat was a real bargain at only thirty dollars.
example1_ru: Это пальто было настоящей находкой всего за тридцать долларов.
example2: The market has excellent bargains on fruit every Friday.

en_definition: a person who takes money from customers in a shop or bank
example1: The cashier was friendly and helped me find the right size.
example1_ru: Кассир была приветливой и помогла мне подобрать нужный размер.
example2: I paid the cashier and received my change.

en_definition: the money used in a particular country
example1: We need to exchange our money for the local currency.
example1_ru: Нам нужно обменять деньги на местную валюту.
example2: The Euro is the official currency of many European countries.

en_definition: the value of one currency compared to another
example1: The exchange rate between dollars and euros changed this week.
example1_ru: Курс обмена долларов на евро изменился на этой неделе.
example2: Check the exchange rate before you travel abroad.

en_definition: money that someone lends you, which you must pay back
example1: He took a small loan to buy a car.
example1_ru: Он взял небольшой кредит, чтобы купить машину.
example2: Many students need a loan to pay for university.

en_definition: a plan for how much money you can spend
example1: I need to make a careful budget to save money each month.
example1_ru: Мне нужно составить тщательный бюджет, чтобы каждый месяц откладывать деньги.
example2: The government announced a new budget for education.

en_definition: feeling as if everything is spinning and you might fall
example1: I felt dizzy after standing up too quickly.
example1_ru: У меня закружилась голова, когда я слишком быстро встал.
example2: The student became dizzy from spinning around.

en_definition: a feeling that you might vomit
example1: She experienced nausea and went to see the doctor.
example1_ru: У неё была тошнота, и она пошла к врачу.
example2: The boat rocked and caused nausea among the passengers.

en_definition: a doctor's written order for medicine that you must take
example1: The doctor gave me a prescription for antibiotics.
example1_ru: Врач выписал мне рецепт на антибиотики.
example2: Take this prescription to the pharmacy to get your medicine.

en_definition: a shop where medicines are sold
example1: I need to go to the pharmacy to pick up my medicine.
example1_ru: Мне нужно зайти в аптеку за лекарством.
example2: The pharmacy is open until nine o'clock in the evening.

en_definition: a hurt or injury on your body caused by cutting or hitting
example1: The nurse cleaned the wound with water and soap.
example1_ru: Медсестра промыла рану водой с мылом.
example2: His knee wound needed stitches after the accident.

en_definition: a continuous pain that is not very strong
example1: I have a muscle ache after exercising yesterday.
example1_ru: После вчерашней тренировки у меня болят мышцы.
example2: An ear ache can be very uncomfortable for children.

en_definition: a bad reaction of your body to something you eat or touch
example1: My son has a peanut allergy and cannot eat nuts.
example1_ru: У моего сына аллергия на арахис, и ему нельзя есть орехи.
example2: Many people have allergies to pollen in spring.

en_definition: to become healthy and strong again after being sick
example1: My grandmother is recovering well after her operation.
example1_ru: Моя бабушка хорошо восстанавливается после операции.
example2: It took him two weeks to recover from the flu.

en_definition: when you get medicine injected to protect you from disease
example1: My baby received his vaccinations at the clinic today.
example1_ru: Сегодня в поликлинике моему малышу сделали прививки.
example2: Vaccination against flu is recommended in winter.

en_definition: a prediction of what will happen in the future, often about weather
example1: The weather forecast says it will rain tomorrow.
example1_ru: По прогнозу погоды, завтра будет дождь.
example2: The forecast predicted cold temperatures for the weekend.

en_definition: a very strong storm with thunder, lightning, and heavy rain
example1: A thunderstorm brought heavy rain to the city last night.
example1_ru: Прошлой ночью гроза принесла в город сильный дождь.
example2: The students stayed inside during the thunderstorm.

en_definition: very light rain with small drops of water
example1: There is just a drizzle, so you do not need an umbrella.
example1_ru: На улице лишь моросит, так что зонт не понадобится.
example2: The morning drizzle made the grass wet.

en_definition: the amount of water in the air
example1: The humidity is very high today and it feels sticky.
example1_ru: Сегодня очень высокая влажность, и на улице липко.
example2: High humidity makes the weather feel hotter than it really is.

en_definition: a thin white layer of ice that forms on the ground at night
example1: The grass was covered with frost this cold morning.
example1_ru: Этим холодным утром трава была покрыта инеем.
example2: Frost kills plants if they are not protected.

en_definition: a light wind that is not very strong
example1: A cool breeze came from the ocean on the hot day.
example1_ru: В жаркий день с океана подул прохладный ветерок.
example2: We sat outside enjoying the pleasant evening breeze.

en_definition: how hot or cold something is, measured in degrees
example1: The temperature dropped below freezing last night.
example1_ru: Прошлой ночью температура опустилась ниже нуля.
example2: Normal body temperature is thirty-seven degrees Celsius.

en_definition: the period of the year when crops are gathered from the fields
example1: Farmers work long hours during harvest time in autumn.
example1_ru: Осенью, во время сбора урожая, фермеры работают допоздна.
example2: The market sells fresh vegetables at harvest time every year.

en_definition: a very large ocean wave caused by an earthquake or volcano
example1: The tsunami caused significant damage to the coastal towns.
example1_ru: Цунами нанесло серьёзный ущерб прибрежным городам.
example2: Scientists warn about tsunami dangers in earthquake zones.

en_definition: a long period of time with no rain
example1: The drought destroyed the crops and caused food shortages.
example1_ru: Засуха уничтожила урожай и вызвала нехватку продовольствия.
example2: The region suffered from a severe drought for two years.

en_definition: a large area of land covered with many trees
example1: We hiked through the forest and saw many animals.
example1_ru: Мы прошли через лес пешком и увидели много животных.
example2: The Amazon forest is home to thousands of species.

en_definition: a large natural flow of water that runs across land
example1: We crossed the river on an old stone bridge.
example1_ru: Мы перешли реку по старому каменному мосту.
example2: The river is deep and dangerous during the rainy season.

en_definition: a field with grass and flowers but no trees
example1: Children played in the sunny meadow near the village.
example1_ru: Дети играли на солнечном лугу возле деревни.
example2: The meadow was full of colorful wildflowers in spring.

en_definition: the time when the sun disappears below the horizon in evening
example1: We watched the beautiful sunset from the beach.
example1_ru: Мы наблюдали красивый закат с пляжа.
example2: The sunset painted the sky with orange and purple colors.

en_definition: a dark area that is made when light is blocked by something
example1: The tall building cast a long shadow on the street.
example1_ru: Высокое здание отбрасывало на улицу длинную тень.
example2: We rested in the shadow of the big tree during lunch.

en_definition: the dark material on the ground where plants grow
example1: The soil in this area is very fertile for farming.
example1_ru: Почва в этой местности очень плодородная для земледелия.
example2: Plants need good soil to grow strong and healthy.

en_definition: to produce flowers and open them
example1: The roses bloom in spring and smell wonderful.
example1_ru: Розы цветут весной и прекрасно пахнут.
example2: The flowers will bloom when the weather gets warmer.

en_definition: the gathering of crops or the crops that are gathered
example1: The farmers worked hard during the harvest season.
example1_ru: Во время сбора урожая фермеры много работали.
example2: This year's harvest was very good because of good weather.

en_definition: to make something very wet by putting it in liquid
example1: The rain soaked our clothes, so we had to change them.
example1_ru: Дождь промочил нашу одежду, так что пришлось переодеться.
example2: Soak the beans in water before cooking them.

en_definition: the highest point of something, especially a mountain
example1: We climbed to the peak of the mountain early in the morning.
example1_ru: Мы поднялись на вершину горы рано утром.
example2: The peak was covered with snow even in summer.

en_definition: a low area of land between hills or mountains
example1: The valley was green and beautiful with many farms.
example1_ru: Долина была зелёной и красивой, с множеством ферм.
example2: A river runs through the valley near the town.

en_definition: an underground railway system that carries passengers in a city
example1: I take the subway to work every morning.
example1_ru: Я езжу на работу на метро каждое утро.
example2: The subway station is five minutes from my apartment.

en_definition: the way you go from one place to another
example1: What is the fastest route to the airport from here?
example1_ru: Какой самый быстрый маршрут отсюда до аэропорта?
example2: The bus route passes near the university campus.

en_definition: a transport service that goes back and forth regularly
example1: The hotel provides a free shuttle to the airport.
example1_ru: Отель предоставляет бесплатный трансфер до аэропорта.
example2: The shuttle bus runs every thirty minutes.

en_definition: to enter a vehicle or board a plane
example1: We got on the train at the main station.
example1_ru: Мы сели на поезд на главной станции.
example2: The passengers got on the bus quickly.

en_definition: to leave a vehicle or plane
example1: We got off the bus at the next stop.
example1_ru: Мы вышли из автобуса на следующей остановке.
example2: All passengers must get off the plane safely.

en_definition: a strap in a vehicle that holds you safely in your seat
example1: Always fasten your seat belt before the car moves.
example1_ru: Всегда пристёгивайте ремень безопасности перед тем, как машина тронется.
example2: The plane required all passengers to wear seat belts.

en_definition: to go faster or increase speed
example1: The driver accelerated when the light turned green.
example1_ru: Водитель нажал на газ, когда загорелся зелёный свет.
example2: The car accelerated quickly on the highway.

en_definition: to make a vehicle slow down or stop
example1: The driver braked suddenly to avoid an accident.
example1_ru: Водитель резко затормозил, чтобы избежать аварии.
example2: Please brake gently at the red light.

en_definition: an accident when two vehicles hit each other
example1: There was a collision on the highway this morning.
example1_ru: Сегодня утром на шоссе произошло столкновение.
example2: The collision caused significant damage to both cars.

en_definition: petrol or other substances used to power vehicles
example1: The car runs out of fuel on the empty road.
example1_ru: У машины закончилось топливо на пустой дороге.
example2: Diesel fuel is more expensive than regular petrol.

en_definition: a large building with many shops inside
example1: We went to the shopping mall on Saturday afternoon.
example1_ru: В субботу днём мы ходили в торговый центр.
example2: The mall has stores for clothes, food, and electronics.

en_definition: a small shop that sells fashionable clothes or accessories
example1: The boutique specializes in Italian designer clothes.
example1_ru: Этот бутик специализируется на итальянской дизайнерской одежде.
example2: This boutique has beautiful handmade jewelry.

en_definition: a private room in a shop where you can try on clothes
example1: The fitting room is at the back of the store.
example1_ru: Примерочная находится в глубине магазина.
example2: Each fitting room can fit one person at a time.

en_definition: the measurement or dimensions of something
example1: What size shoes do you wear?
example1_ru: Какой у вас размер обуви?
example2: This shirt is available in three sizes: small, medium, and large.

en_definition: the name of a company that makes products
example1: Nike is a famous athletic shoe brand.
example1_ru: Nike — известный бренд спортивной обуви.
example2: I prefer this brand of coffee because it tastes good.

en_definition: how good or bad something is
example1: This furniture is of high quality and will last many years.
example1_ru: Эта мебель высокого качества и прослужит много лет.
example2: The quality of the fabric is poor, so it tears easily.

en_definition: to negotiate the price of something
example1: At the market, customers bargain for lower prices.
example1_ru: На рынке покупатели торгуются, чтобы сбить цену.
example2: I bargained for a better price and saved fifty dollars.

en_definition: a label showing the price of something in a shop
example1: The price tag on the sweater says twenty-five dollars.
example1_ru: На ценнике свитера написано двадцать пять долларов.
example2: I could not see the price tag on this item.

en_definition: a paper that gives a discount on a product
example1: I used a coupon to get ten percent off my purchase.
example1_ru: Я использовал купон, чтобы получить скидку десять процентов на покупку.
example2: The store prints coupons in their weekly newspaper.

en_definition: a container on wheels that you push to carry items while shopping
example1: I pushed the cart around the supermarket.
example1_ru: Я катил тележку по супермаркету.
example2: The shopping cart was full of groceries.

en_definition: a plastic card used to buy things and pay later
example1: I paid with my credit card at the checkout.
example1_ru: Я расплатился кредитной картой на кассе.
example2: Many shops accept credit cards as payment.

en_definition: a card that takes money directly from your bank account
example1: I use my debit card to withdraw money from the ATM.
example1_ru: Я снимаю деньги в банкомате с помощью дебетовой карты.
example2: Debit cards are safer than carrying cash.

en_definition: to take money out of your bank account
example1: I need to withdraw some cash from the bank.
example1_ru: Мне нужно снять немного наличных в банке.
example2: You can withdraw money from any ATM machine.

en_definition: an amount of money you owe the bank after spending more than you have
example1: She had to pay a fee because of her bank overdraft.
example1_ru: Ей пришлось заплатить комиссию из-за овердрафта по счёту.
example2: He avoided an overdraft by checking his balance every week.

en_definition: money used to buy something that will make more money
example1: Investing in real estate is a good investment.
example1_ru: Инвестиции в недвижимость — хорошее вложение.
example2: He made a wise investment in the technology company.

en_definition: money you earn for working
example1: My salary is paid every month by direct bank transfer.
example1_ru: Моя зарплата выплачивается каждый месяц прямым переводом на счёт.
example2: The salary for this job is competitive.

en_definition: money paid to the government for public services
example1: Income tax is taken from my monthly salary.
example1_ru: Подоходный налог удерживается из моей ежемесячной зарплаты.
example2: Sales tax is added to the price of most items.

en_definition: a protection against loss or damage that you pay for
example1: Car insurance is required by law in most countries.
example1_ru: В большинстве стран автострахование обязательно по закону.
example2: I have health insurance through my employer.

en_definition: money you give to someone for good service
example1: I left a good tip for the waiter at the restaurant.
example1_ru: Я оставил официанту в ресторане хорошие чаевые.
example2: A fifteen percent tip is customary in many places.

en_definition: money that you owe to someone
example1: He is trying to pay off his credit card debt.
example1_ru: Он пытается погасить долг по кредитной карте.
example2: Student loans can create significant debt.

en_definition: to suddenly push air out through your nose and mouth
example1: The dust made me sneeze several times.
example1_ru: От пыли я несколько раз чихнул.
example2: Cover your mouth when you sneeze.

en_definition: to suddenly push air out of your lungs with a harsh sound
example1: The smoke made everyone in the room cough.
example1_ru: Дым заставил всех в комнате закашляться.
example2: Stop coughing and drink some water.

en_definition: pain or discomfort in your throat
example1: I have a sore throat and need to see the doctor.
example1_ru: У меня болит горло, нужно сходить к врачу.
example2: Drinking warm tea helps a sore throat feel better.

en_definition: sudden feelings of cold and shaking
example1: High fever and chills are signs of the flu.
example1_ru: Высокая температура и озноб — признаки гриппа.
example2: The patient complained of chills and body aches.

en_definition: bigger than normal due to injury or illness
example1: My ankle is swollen after I twisted it.
example1_ru: После того как я подвернул лодыжку, она опухла.
example2: His throat was swollen and he could not swallow.

en_definition: a dark mark on your skin from being hit or bumped
example1: She had a large bruise on her arm after falling.
example1_ru: После падения у неё на руке остался большой синяк.
example2: The bruise turned purple and then yellow as it healed.

en_definition: a small pocket of fluid that forms on your skin
example1: New shoes gave me a blister on my heel.
example1_ru: Новые туфли натёрли мне мозоль на пятке.
example2: Do not pop the blister or it might get infected.

en_definition: to injure a muscle by using it too hard
example1: I strained my back lifting heavy boxes.
example1_ru: Я потянул спину, поднимая тяжёлые коробки.
example2: Be careful not to strain your neck during exercise.

en_definition: to lose consciousness for a short time
example1: She felt dizzy and began to faint.
example1_ru: У неё закружилась голова, и она начала терять сознание.
example2: The patient might faint from the pain.

en_definition: an injury to a joint when a ligament is stretched or torn
example1: The football player had a sprain in his ankle.
example1_ru: У футболиста было растяжение лодыжки.
example2: A severe sprain may need several weeks to heal.

en_definition: a heavy snowstorm with strong wind
example1: The blizzard closed all the roads in the area.
example1_ru: Из-за метели все дороги в округе были закрыты.
example2: It was impossible to see in the blizzard.

en_definition: small balls of ice that fall from the sky during storms
example1: The hail damaged crops and broke windows.
example1_ru: Град повредил посевы и разбил окна.
example2: Large hail fell during the summer thunderstorm.

en_definition: a sudden strong movement of wind
example1: A gust of wind blew my hat off my head.
example1_ru: Порыв ветра сдул с меня шляпу.
example2: Strong gusts made sailing dangerous on the water.

en_definition: when the weather becomes better and the sky becomes clear
example1: The rain will clear up this afternoon.
example1_ru: Сегодня днём дождь прекратится и прояснится.
example2: Let us wait for the weather to clear up before we leave.

ru_translation: идти дождем, лить как из ведра
en_definition: to rain very heavily
example1: It was pouring down all day, so we stayed inside.
example1_ru: Весь день лило как из ведра, поэтому мы остались дома.
example2: The rain poured down and flooded the streets.

en_definition: having a lot of water in the air
example1: The weather is hot and humid in summer.
example1_ru: Летом погода жаркая и влажная.
example2: Humid air makes your clothes feel sticky.

en_definition: a bright flash of electricity in the sky during a storm
example1: The lightning was very bright during the storm.
example1_ru: Во время грозы молния была очень яркой.
example2: Lightning struck the tree during the thunderstorm.

en_definition: to dry up or become weak
example1: The flowers began to wither without water.
example1_ru: Цветы начали увядать без воды.
example2: Plants wither in extreme heat.

en_definition: uncomfortably warm and damp in a way that makes you sweat
example1: The muggy afternoon made everyone want to stay indoors.
example1_ru: Душный день заставил всех остаться дома.
example2: It felt muggy near the river after the rain stopped.

en_definition: related to the coast or shore of the sea
example1: Coastal cities have beautiful beaches and sea views.
example1_ru: В прибрежных городах красивые пляжи и вид на море.
example2: The coastal region is known for fishing and tourism.
